name: ADA 2024 T2DM First-Line Treatment
query: What are the first-line treatments for Type 2 diabetes per ADA 2024 guidelines?
reference_answer: Per the 2024 ADA Standards of Care, first-line pharmacotherapy for Type 2 diabetes includes metformin (if tolerated and no contraindications), GLP-1 receptor agonists (especially with established cardiovascular disease or high CV risk), and SGLT-2 inhibitors (particularly when heart failure or CKD is present). Lifestyle modification (diet and exercise) remains the cornerstone of all management. Agent-agnostic glycemic control, weight management, and cardiorenal protection drive agent selection.
pass_criteria: Response mentions metformin AND at least one of GLP-1 or SGLT2 in context of ADA 2024 recommendations
notes: Tests ADA 2024 guideline retrieval and first-line pharmacotherapy knowledge

name: Essential Hypertension ICD-10 Code
query: What ICD-10 code applies to essential hypertension?
reference_answer: The ICD-10-CM code for essential (primary) hypertension is I10. This code covers hypertension not attributable to a secondary cause. When hypertension occurs with chronic kidney disease, use combination codes I12.x (hypertensive CKD); with chronic heart failure, use I11.x (hypertensive heart disease); with both CKD and heart failure, use I13.x. ICD-10-CM Official Guidelines Section I.C.9.a govern hypertension coding.
pass_criteria: Response explicitly includes ICD-10 code I10
notes: Tests ICD-10 lookup for the most common outpatient primary diagnosis

name: Medicare Part B CGM Coverage 2024
query: Does Medicare Part B cover CGM devices in 2024?
reference_answer: Yes. Medicare Part B covers therapeutic continuous glucose monitors (CGMs) under the DMEPOS benefit. Following the 2023 CMS policy expansion, coverage no longer requires insulin use. Governing policy: LCD L33822 (Noridian) and related contractor LCDs. HCPCS codes include E2101 (CGM receiver/display device), A4238/A4239 (CGM supplies/sensors), K0553/K0554 (therapeutic CGM categories). Documentation must establish medical necessity for diabetes management, and the ordering physician must treat the beneficiary's diabetes.
pass_criteria: Response mentions LCD policy reference AND therapeutic CGM coverage expansion (insulin requirement removed)
notes: Tests CMS coverage policy retrieval and HCPCS code awareness for a common RCM scenario

name: PubMed Evidence: SGLT2 Inhibitors and Heart Failure
query: Summarize recent PubMed evidence on SGLT2 inhibitors and heart failure
reference_answer: SGLT2 inhibitors show robust benefit in heart failure across landmark trials. EMPEROR-Reduced (PMID 32865375, NEJM 2020): empagliflozin reduced composite CV death or HF hospitalization by 25% in HFrEF. DAPA-HF (PMID 31535829, NEJM 2019): dapagliflozin reduced worsening HF or CV death by 26% in HFrEF. EMPEROR-Preserved (PMID 34449189): empagliflozin demonstrated benefit in HFpEF. Both empagliflozin and dapagliflozin are now FDA-approved for HFrEF regardless of diabetes status.
pass_criteria: Response cites at least 1 PMID from a relevant cardiovascular outcomes trial
notes: Tests PubMed retrieval and citation of landmark cardiovascular RCTs

name: CPT Code: New Patient Office Visit, Moderate Complexity
query: What CPT code is used for a new patient office visit, moderate complexity?
reference_answer: CPT code 99204 covers a new patient office or outpatient visit with moderate medical decision making (MDM) or 45 minutes of total physician time. CPT 99205 covers a new patient visit with high MDM or 60 minutes of total time. Per the 2021 AMA E/M revisions, history and physical exam are no longer required elements; MDM complexity or total time determines the level. Note: CPT codes are proprietary to the AMA and require a license for redistribution.
pass_criteria: Response includes CPT 99204 or 99205 with supporting E/M coding context
notes: Tests E/M coding knowledge; agent should acknowledge AMA CPT licensing restrictions
